message:
test(readme): readme_groupby_aggregation_matrix_compiles_and_runs (fd90.179 / frankenpandas-zl3fl)

Implements the integration test registered in
frankenpandas-zl3fl. Mirrors the README's "GroupBy: Complete
Aggregation Matrix" section (lines 545-566) end-to-end so a
regression in any of the documented DataFrameGroupBy surface paths
surfaces as a test failure rather than broken docs.

Coverage:

- Constructs a deterministic 6-row DataFrame via read_csv_str with
  two equal-sized groups (a=1: rows 0/1/2 with b values 10/20/30;
  a=2: rows 3/4/5 with b values 40/50/60). Equal group sizes mean
  none of the aggregations can incidentally pass via "uses first
  row only" or "uses N=1" shortcuts.
- Direct method calls returning a DataFrame indexed by group keys:
  sum, mean, count, min, max, std, var, median, first, last, prod,
  size, nunique, idxmin, idxmax, all, any.
- agg() string dispatch covering the full 14-name table from the
  README: sum / mean / count / min / max / std / var / median /
  first / last / prod / sem / skew / kurt / kurtosis. Round-trips
  the README's documented string surface so a typo or rename in
  the dispatcher fails the test loudly.
- Group-level ops returning per-row results: cumsum, cumprod,
  cummax, cummin, rank, shift, diff, head, tail, value_counts,
  describe, get_group, cumcount, ngroup, ohlc.
- Multi-aggregation: agg(&[fns]) — list-form returning one column
  per agg — and agg_named(&[(name, src, fn)]) — named-output form.

Pure additive test; uses prelude-only imports per the existing file
convention. No source/library changes.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
